FIG.title: Figma Stock Jumps On Q1 Earnings Beat As Customers Go 'Bigger And Broader'

COIN.title: CLARITY Act Clears Senate Banking Committee, But Prediction Markets Slam The Breaks On The Euphoria

HOOD.title: CLARITY Act Clears Senate Banking Committee, But Prediction Markets Slam The Breaks On The Euphoria

MSTR.title: CLARITY Act Clears Senate Banking Committee, But Prediction Markets Slam The Breaks On The Euphoria

macro_events.event_summary: Bond market signals the Fed is behind the curve on inflation as Kevin Warsh (a known hawk) takes over the Fed chairmanship, implying higher-for-longer rates and a steeper curve.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields / bear steepening as inflation expectations un-anchor
Hawkish regime shift under Warsh — perceived tighter policy reaction function
Rising real rates compress long-duration equity valuations

beneficiaries.rationale: Large-cap bank with asset-sensitive balance sheet; steeper curve and higher reinvestment yields on securities book lift NII. Also a flight-to-quality bank if smaller banks wobble.
Rate volatility and repositioning around a new Fed chair drives record interest-rate futures/options volumes; CME's rate complex is direct revenue beneficiary of curve repricing.
Higher short rates lift net interest revenue on client cash sweeps; also benefits from elevated trading activity around Fed regime change.
Inflation-hedge proxy via energy; 'Fed behind the curve' narrative historically correlates with commodity bid as real assets outperform financial assets.
Gold miner — rising inflation expectations with Fed credibility concerns is the textbook setup for gold outperformance; Newmont is the highest-beta liquid pure-play.

losers.rationale: High-multiple software name with valuation almost entirely in terminal-value cash flows; acute duration risk on a hawkish regime change.
Realty Income — bond-proxy REIT with long-lease duration. Rising long yields directly compete with its dividend yield and pressure cap rates.
Highly levered consumer finance-adjacent name; higher funding costs and consumer rate pressure compound. Squeezed on both balance sheet and demand sides.

headlines: Bond market believes Fed behind the curve on inflation as Warsh takes over
